title: Aureline docs / Help / About / service-health destination descriptor and handoff-route vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the destination_descriptor_record frozen in docs/docs/help_about_service_health_routes.md. Every Help, About, service-health, docs-browser, migration-center, onboarding, provenance, community-handoff, and support-export route reads one shared destination descriptor rather than inventing surface-local trust badges, auth copy, open-in-browser wording, or offline/mirror semantics. Raw URLs, raw issue-template bodies, raw support-bundle payloads, and raw embedded content bodies never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs and typed vocabulary only. The eventual docs-help-service-health crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new purpose class, trust class, owner class, boundary class, exact-build applicability, availability state, locale-availability class, offline behavior, route class, external-open policy, auth expectation, issue-template support state, data-exit boundary, disclosure-safety class, or destination-manifest source kind is additive-minor and requires a destination_descriptor_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

destination_descriptor_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this destination-descriptor export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, support exports, release packets, and evidence bundles.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

bcp47_locale_tag.description: BCP-47 language tag (for example en, en-US, pt-BR, zh-Hant-HK). Case is normalized by the producer but preserved here for round-trip compatibility.

surface_family.description: Closed surface-family vocabulary naming which product or export surfaces consume the descriptor.

purpose_class.description: Closed purpose-class vocabulary. One descriptor family serves docs, service-health, About/provenance, support, migration, onboarding, and community routes without branching into surface-local copies.

destination_trust_class.description: Closed trust-class vocabulary for the destination itself. Mirrors the product language frozen in the technical design document: official public, official authenticated, community, and local only remain distinct in UI and exports.

owner_class.description: Closed owner-class vocabulary naming who owns the destination on the other side of the route or who owns the local artifact when the route never leaves the machine.

boundary_class.description: Closed boundary-class vocabulary naming where bytes, authority, and state live once the route is followed.

disclosure_mode.description: How ownership and boundary must be disclosed. Local and offline-cached routes disclose inline; embedded routes use the host-owned boundary card (ADR-0015); browser and device-code routes disclose before leaving the product.

source_descriptor_kind.description: Closed source-descriptor-kind vocabulary naming which upstream record family published this descriptor.

exact_build_applicability.description: Closed exact-build-applicability vocabulary. same_build_only is the default interpretation when no wider compatibility window is published. not_build_bound is reserved for routes whose truth is independent of the running build and therefore does not compute a version-match state.

version_match_state.description: Re-exported from schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json without modification. Computed against the running build's exact-build identity whenever exact_build_applicability is not_build_bound.

support_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification. Support class stays a separate axis from trust class, freshness, and client scope.

availability_state.description: Closed availability-state vocabulary. Freshness answers how current a descriptor is; availability answers whether and how the route can currently be used.

locale_availability_class.description: Closed locale-availability vocabulary for destination rows. not_applicable is used for routes whose payload is language-neutral or whose locale is not part of the contract.

offline_behavior.description: Closed offline-behavior vocabulary. Surfaces use this together with freshness and availability so offline, cached, mirrored, and local-export routes do not overclaim live truth.

route_class.description: Closed route-class vocabulary. Every seeded descriptor names one primary route and zero-or-more fallback routes from this set.

external_destination_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json without modification. Present when the descriptor may hand off to a browser or device-code destination.

external_open_policy.description: Closed open-in-browser policy vocabulary. required_primary_route means the route is browser-first. required_fallback_when_in_product_unavailable covers embedded or cached routes whose truthful fallback is browser-only.

auth_expectation.description: Closed auth-expectation vocabulary. Auth expectation is distinct from trust class and distinct from whether the route is local, embedded, browser-mediated, or device-code mediated.

browser_handoff_reason_subset.description: Re-exported from the subset frozen in schemas/docs/help_status_badge.schema.json. This subset is the only handoff-reason vocabulary docs/help/About/service-health/support routes may quote because it remains screenshot-safe and export-safe.

disclosure_safety_class.description: Closed disclosure-safety vocabulary for route reasons. Browser and device-code handoff reasons used by this descriptor family MUST remain screenshot-safe and export-safe.

issue_template_support.description: Closed issue-template support vocabulary. local_export_packet keeps the payload on-device; external_prefill opens a browser route with safe metadata only; local_export_and_external_prefill supports both.

data_exit_boundary.description: Closed data-exit boundary vocabulary. Surfaces disclose this field before navigation or export so users can tell whether nothing leaves the machine, only metadata leaves, or a reviewed export packet or user-authored submission is required.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the descriptor was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the descriptor was minted inside a live execution. Null when minted outside an execution context.

policy_context.description: Policy context the descriptor was minted under. ADR-0008 / ADR-0009 policy epoch and ADR-0001 trust state are re-validated on every render.

title.description: Short human-legible title for the destination row or exportable handoff tile.

purpose_summary.description: Copy-safe summary of why the route exists and what the user should expect from it.

surface_families.description: Surface families that consume the descriptor directly instead of rewriting its route vocabulary.

source_ref.description: Opaque stable ref to the source object that published the descriptor.

source_revision_ref.description: Opaque revision or manifest pin for the source object. Null only when the source kind has no versioned revision.

display_source_version.description: Human-legible version or channel string safe to render in product or export surfaces.

running_build_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the running build's exact-build identity when this descriptor is build-bound. Null when exact_build_applicability = not_build_bound.

version_match_state.description: Version-match state against the running build. Null only when exact_build_applicability = not_build_bound.

client_scopes.description: Client classes this descriptor is published for. A client whose scope is excluded MUST NOT render the route as available there.

available_locales.description: Locales the destination advertises directly. Empty only when locale_availability_class = not_applicable.

fallback_route_classes.description: Zero-or-more fallback routes the surface may offer without changing the meaning of the descriptor.

external_destination_class.description: Browser or device-code destination class when the descriptor may leave the product. Null when external_open_policy = not_permitted and no fallback leaves the product.

browser_handoff_reason.description: Reason code used when the descriptor hands off to a browser or device-code route. Null when no handoff route exists.

disclosure_safety_class.description: Safety posture for the user-visible handoff reason. Browser and device-code routes on this descriptor family must remain screenshot-safe and export-safe.

last_refreshed_at.description: Monotonic timestamp of the last refresh against the descriptor's canonical owner. Null when the route is packaged locally and has no live refresh path.

allOf.description: Build-bound descriptors MUST carry a running_build_identity_ref and a version_match_state.
not_build_bound descriptors MUST keep build/version fields null.
Locale-neutral descriptors MUST keep available_locales empty.
Localized descriptors MUST name at least one locale.
Embedded routes use the embedded boundary card disclosure mode.
Browser and device-code routes disclose before leaving the product.
Local and offline-cached routes disclose inline.
When a handoff reason is present, the descriptor MUST name the destination class and keep the reason screenshot-safe and export-safe.
Version-mismatched descriptors MUST compute a non-green version-match state.

destination_descriptor_record.description: Shared destination descriptor consumed by Help, About, service-health, docs-browser, migration, onboarding, provenance, community-handoff, and support-export surfaces. Product-bound fields (trust class, owner class, boundary class, source/version, exact-build applicability, support class, client scope, freshness, availability, route class, auth expectation, issue-template support, data-exit boundary, and handoff safety) remain separately addressable so UI, CLI, exports, and evidence packets can reuse one vocabulary without rewriting route semantics.
